sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root נָזַר) consecrated, specially,

(1) a Nazarite, a kind of ascetic among the Hebrews, who by vow abstained from certain things (see the law, Num 6:13, seqq.), Amo 2:11, 12 more fully נְזִיר אֱלֹהִים consecrated to God, Jud. 13:5 , Jdg 13:7 16:17 . The word has been applied from a Nazarite who did not shave his hair, to a vine, which in every seventh, and also in every fiftieth year was not pruned, Lev 25:5, 11 Lev 25:11, compare Lat. herba virgo, and Talmud. בתולת שׁקמה virginity of a sycamore, used of a sycamore not yet pruned.

(2) a prince, as being consecrated to God, Gen 49:26 Deu 33:16 Lam 4:7, compare מָשִׁיהַ.
